home.title: CESeg · Special Committee on Cybersecurity (SBC)
home.desc: The Special Committee on Cybersecurity of the Brazilian Computer Society brings together and articulates the Brazilian academic community in cybersecurity.
home.lead: Bringing together and articulating the Brazilian academic community working in cybersecurity.
home.aboutText: The Special Committee on Cybersecurity (CESeg) of the Brazilian Computer Society brings together and articulates the Brazilian academic community working in cybersecurity. Established in 2004, it gathers researchers, students, industry and professionals around the science and technology of cybersecurity in Brazil.
home.eventText: Brazilian Symposium on Cybersecurity: the forum that connects research, industry and students.

lista.title: Mailing List · CESeg
lista.desc: Mailing list of the SBC cybersecurity community.
lista.intro: The SBC Information and Computer Systems Security mailing list is a meeting point for the Brazilian academic community working in security, with thematic discussions and event announcements.
lista.warn: This list is not meant to be used for technical consulting on system configuration, there are more appropriate forums for that.
lista.joinText: Subscription and archive access (for subscribers) are handled through the list platform, hosted at UFRGS.

org.title: Organization · CESeg
org.desc: Coordination and steering committee of CESeg.
org.intro: CESeg is led by a team elected by the community, supported by a steering committee.

comissoes.title: Committees · CESeg
comissoes.intro: The committees organize specific lines of work within CESeg.
comissoes.eduText: Appointed by Ordinance No. 020 of July 23, 2021, it produced the cybersecurity curricular references intended for the National Curricular Guidelines. The public consultation ran from 12/13/2022 to 02/27/2023, and the final version was published in the SBC Digital Library (SOL).
comissoes.compText: Established on September 19, 2023, it works to define and promote competition strategies aligned with the Education Committee, serving the security research community and society's demands.

conferencistas.title: Senior Speakers · CESeg
conferencistas.desc: Senior speakers of the SBC cybersecurity community.
conferencistas.intro: Senior researchers recognized by the SBC cybersecurity community.
conferencistas.progText: In 2014, the Brazilian Computer Society (SBC) launched the Senior Speakers Program. Through it, each Special Committee nominates researchers with extensive experience in their field to represent it at events and lectures.

grupos.title: Research Groups · CESeg
grupos.intro: Research groups in systems, network and information security, spread across universities throughout Brazil.
grupos.liveText: The list of research groups is dynamic and maintained on the official CESeg portal, where it is continuously updated. See the complete list, institutions, coordinators and lines of work, directly at the source:
grupos.liveCta: See the full list of groups →

instituto.title: Institute · CESeg
instituto.desc: National Institute of Science and Technology in Cybersecurity.
instituto.intro: A collaboration among the country's leading research institutions, articulated under the National Institutes of Science and Technology program (INCT/CNPq), focused on cybersecurity.
instituto.fullName: National Institute of Science and Technology in Cybersecurity
instituto.netText: A network of 19 research institutions, led by UNICAMP, dedicated to the science and technology of cybersecurity.

sbseg.title: SBSeg · CESeg
sbseg.intro: SBSeg is the leading scientific event on information and computer systems security in Brazil, promoted by SBC through CESeg.
sbseg.histText: Formerly called the Brazilian Symposium on Information and Computer Systems Security, SBSeg ran as a workshop from 2001 to 2004. From 2005, alongside the creation of the special committee, it began to be organized as a standalone event, becoming the country's leading cybersecurity forum.
sbseg.olderText: The proceedings of all editions are available in the SBC Digital Library (SOL).
sbseg.photosNote: Offline clones preserved by the SBSeg archive; event photos on Facebook (facebook.com/SBSegSBC) and Instagram (@sbseg.sbc).
sbseg.histNote: The 2001–2004 editions were held as the Workshop on Computer Systems Security (WSeg).

anais.title: Proceedings · Main Track · CESeg
anais.desc: Proceedings of the SBSeg main track.
anais.intro: Proceedings of the main track of the Brazilian Symposium on Cybersecurity, with the scientific papers from the event's editions.
anais.solText: The Brazilian Computer Society Digital Library (SOL) gathers the papers from all SBSeg editions.

minicursos.title: Short Courses · CESeg
minicursos.intro: The SBSeg short courses offer in-depth training on current cybersecurity topics, published as book chapters.
minicursos.solText: The SBSeg short courses are published as book chapters and are available in the SBC Digital Library (SOL).
minicursos.ebooksText: The short courses presented at each SBSeg edition were compiled and published as an ebook in the SBC OpenLib catalog. Access the ebook for each edition:
minicursos.searchPlaceholder: Search by edition, title, author or keyword…

wise.title: WISE · CESeg
wise.desc: SBSeg track on information security education.
wise.intro: A track dedicated to education and training in information security, within the SBSeg context.
wise.text: WISE is the SBSeg track dedicated to education and training in information security, gathering works and experiences on teaching cybersecurity.

homenageados.title: Honors · CESeg
homenageados.intro: Since 2018, every two years CESeg brings together the Brazilian cybersecurity community to nominate a researcher to be honored for their contributions to the field. Although the nomination criteria may vary from edition to edition, honorees are generally chosen for their research contributions, their role in training new researchers, and their work on behalf of cybersecurity in Brazil and worldwide. The recognition, named the "Prêmio Destaque CESeg", takes place in a dedicated session during that year's SBSeg.

publicacoes.title: Publications · CESeg
publicacoes.desc: Publications and venues of the cybersecurity community.
publicacoes.intro: References and publication venues relevant to the Brazilian cybersecurity community.
publicacoes.text: This page brings together the complete catalog of SBSeg proceedings published in the SOL/SBC Digital Library, plus complementary indexes and search tools that make it easy to locate and browse across editions, by title, author or topic.
publicacoes.ebooksCardNote: Each SBSeg edition's short courses, compiled and published as an ebook in the SBC OpenLib catalog.
publicacoes.solText: The proceedings of every SBSeg edition are published in SBC's SOL digital library. The resources on this page complement that official archive with search and indexes that span all editions.
publicacoes.cloudNote: Most recurring themes across all SBSeg editions, combining the Main Track and the Extended Annals. Keywords were automatically extracted from paper titles. Click a term to search for it in the proceedings.

referenciais.title: References · CESeg
referenciais.intro: Reference materials for research and teaching in cybersecurity.
referenciais.text: The Cybersecurity Training References were developed by CESeg's Education Committee to guide institutions on the competencies required to train professionals in the field, aiming at integration with the National Curricular Guidelines (DCN) of the Ministry of Education. The final version is published in the SBC Digital Library (SOL). © 2020 CESeg.

ondepublicar.title: Where to Publish · CESeg
ondepublicar.desc: Journals and conferences for publishing in the field.
ondepublicar.intro: Journals and conferences for publishing cybersecurity research.
ondepublicar.cNote: Among other leading international conferences in the field.

anaistp.note: All SBSeg Main Track papers, grouped by edition. Use the search box to filter by title, author or keyword.
anaistp.tpcCallout: Who on the Program Committee publishes in the Main Track? See the cross-reference between TPC members and accepted papers in each edition.

anaisest.note: All SBSeg Extended Annals papers, grouped by edition and sub-event (workshops and tracks). Use the search box to filter by title, author or keyword.

mapForm.intro: Are you a researcher, or do you lead a cybersecurity group or program? Send your details so we can add you to the map. The form opens your email app with a ready-to-send message.

tpc.title: TPC Statistics · Main Track · CESeg
tpc.desc: Cross-reference between SBSeg Main Track Program Committee (TPC) members and the papers accepted in each edition.
tpc.h1: TPC × Accepted Papers: the Program Committee’s footprint
tpc.lead: Cross-reference between the Technical Program Committee (TPC) members of the SBSeg Main Track and the papers actually accepted in each edition.
tpc.intro: For each edition we cross the TPC roster (taken from that year’s call for papers) with the authorship of the papers accepted in the same edition. The goal is to gauge the weight of the committee’s own submissions, the relationship between TPC size and the volume of accepted papers, and who the most-published members are.
tpc.corrDesc: Editions with larger committees tend to record more accepted papers. The correlation is strong and positive, but does not imply causation: committees grow along with the community and the number of submissions.
tpc.corrAria: Scatter plot: TPC size versus accepted papers per edition
tpc.corrCaption: Each point is an edition. Pearson correlation r =
tpc.edDesc: "Papers with a TPC author" counts how many accepted papers have at least one coauthor who served on the committee that year. "Members who published" counts how many TPC members signed at least one paper. Click "show all" for each edition’s full list.
tpc.rkTitle: Overall ranking of the most-published TPC members
tpc.rkDesc: Sum of accepted papers per person, counting only the editions in which they served on the TPC. A single paper with several committee coauthors is credited to each of them.
tpc.meth1: Papers: main track (full + short) from scripts/sbseg_data.json, extracted from the SOL/SBC proceedings. Openings and presentations do not count.
tpc.meth2: TPC: rosters extracted from each edition’s Main Track call for papers (official sites and clones at sbseg-sbc.github.io; via the Wayback Machine when needed). Program committee members and chairs are included; local organizing and satellite-workshop committees are excluded.
tpc.meth3: Name matching (first + last token, handling suffixes such as Jr./Filho), the same criterion used in the site’s other statistics. Namesakes may cause small inaccuracies.
tpc.meth4: Coverage: 13 editions (2012–2014 and 2016–2025). The 2011 (incomplete roster) and 2015 (roster unavailable online) editions were excluded from the statistics.
